question: According to Girls Who Code, how long did it take in years for the percentage of computer scientists that were women to change by 13% from a starting point of 37%?
aria_reasoning: The task requires the agent to calculate the change in percentage of women computer scientists over time and determine the year when the change occurred. However, the critic scores indicate that the agent failed to satisfy two out of three requirements, including calculating the change in percentage and determining the year when the change occurred. The trace summary shows that the agent performed
requirement_checklist: calculate the change in percentage of women computer scientists
identify the starting point of the percentage
determine the year when the change occurred
executor_output: FINAL ANSWER: According to Girls Who Code, the percentage of women computer scientists has declined over time. In 1995, it was 37%, and as of 2018, it is approximately 33%. If no action is taken, this number will continue to decline.

tool_args.query: percentage of women computer scientists over time Girls Who Code
